title: JPMorganChase inks deal to become the main tenant of new South Station Tower
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: JPMorganChase will move to South Station Tower in 2028, becoming the anchor tenant with 250,000 square feet of space.
The firm plans to add 300 jobs, bringing its total number of Boston employees to approximately 1,600.
South Station Tower, owned by Hines, features 680,000 square feet of office space and Ritz-Carlton condominiums.

title: Here’s the versatile wide receiver Mel Kiper has the Patriots picking 31st overall in his latest mock draft
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Mel Kiper Jr. predicts the Patriots will select wide receiver K.C. Concepcion 31st overall in the NFL Draft.
Concepcion recorded 61 catches for 919 yards and nine touchdowns at Texas A&M in 2025.
Kiper highlights Concepcion's versatility in the passing and return game as valuable for the Patriots.

title: ‘It feels like old times’: As Jayson Tatum’s minutes ramp up, so does chemistry with Jaylen Brown
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jayson Tatum has been increasing his playing minutes as he returns from a ruptured Achilles, contributing to the Celtics' recent success.
Jaylen Brown has been performing at a career-high level, including a 41-point game, as Tatum readjusts to game play.
The Celtics have won four out of six games since Tatum's return, with Tatum and Brown's chemistry aiding their performance.

title: Mass. man gets 10 years for child porn possession after Google reports him
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Scott Morrill of Uxbridge sentenced to 10 years for child pornography possession after Google's report.
Morrill used a Google account to store hundreds of illicit images later reported to law enforcement.
He was previously convicted in 2013 for distributing child pornography and was on supervised release.

title: Local obituary: Matthew McTiernan, 22, Marshfield native and Johnson & Wales student
author: Boston.com Staff
bullets: Matthew McTiernan, 22, a Marshfield native and Johnson & Wales student, died in a car accident on March 11, 2026.
McTiernan was set to graduate with a Bachelor's in Baking and Pastry Arts, known for his love of baking sourdough.
In memory, a Marshfield Youth Football scholarship fund was established, supporting children regardless of financial ability.

title: After serving prison time, Andrea James became a leading criminal justice advocate. Now she’s running for governor.
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Andrea James, a criminal justice advocate, is running for governor of Massachusetts as an independent.
James was previously a real estate attorney, disbarred after a wire fraud conviction, and served two years in prison.
Her platform emphasizes people’s needs over profit, focusing on issues like transparency, healthcare, and opposition to prison construction.

title: ‘The sun will rise, the sun will set, and I’ll have lunch’: The timeless wisdom of Lou Gorman
author: Chad Finn
bullets: Former Red Sox GM Lou Gorman's calm response to Roger Clemens' 1987 contract dispute became a memorable sports quote.
Roger Clemens left spring training in 1987 over a contract issue, causing Gorman's lighthearted comments to go viral.
Clemens eventually returned and won a second Cy Young Award, proving Gorman's relaxed approach successful.

title: She’s running the Boston Marathon for her former patient with Morquio syndrome
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Erin Donnelly, 30, from Boston is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for her late patient, Giovanni Thompson.
Giovanni 'Gio' Thompson had Morquio syndrome and inspired Donnelly during their six-month therapy at Franciscan Children’s.
Gio's spirit and resilience left a lasting impact on Donnelly, motivating her marathon participation in his honor.

title: Bruins fall to Devils 4-3 in OT as US Olympic hero Jack Hughes has 3 assists
author: The Associated Press
bullets: New Jersey Devils defeated the Boston Bruins 4-3 in overtime, with Jack Hughes contributing three assists.
Paul Cotter scored the game-winning goal in overtime for the Devils, adding to his earlier goal in the third period.
David Pastrnak scored twice for the Bruins, who are struggling to secure a wild-card playoff spot in the Eastern Conference.

title: Tyngsborough man charged with murder after human remains found buried under garage floor, police say
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Shawn Sullivan, 40, charged with murder after human remains found under his Tyngsborough garage, believed to be missing Lowell woman.
Jill T. Kloppenburg, 47, was reported missing in February 2025; last seen leaving her Lowell home on January 2, 2025.
Investigators found a bag with human remains after cutting through the garage floor; identification and cause of death underway.
